notes.label: Egg
notes.note: There is no nutritional difference between white and brown eggs. Both provide identical levels of protein, vitamins, and minerals. The shell color is determined solely by the breed of the hen.

notes.label: Pork Zongzi
notes.note: Pork Zongzi contains salted duck egg yolk. This is a Cantonese-style Zongzi served in Hong Kong and Guangdong.

notes.label: Peanut Butter
notes.note:
Peanut butter tips collection: measure it for your diet without messy tablespoon cleanup, have options on what to pair peanut butter with, and know how to dip sanitarily.

Measure peanut butter with less cleanup

Measuring peanut butter with a tablespoon can be annoying because it sticks inside the spoon and is hard to clean since a tablespoon is deep and concave.

A simpler method is to use your regular eating spoon, which is usually more shallow and less concave. Ahead of time, figure out how much peanut butter from your regular spoon equals 1 tablespoon. You can do this by scooping peanut butter with your regular spoon, then transferring it into a measuring tablespoon. You only ever have to do this once for your regular spoon.

For example, with my regular spoon, 2 lightly heaping spoonfuls — with the peanut butter raised no more than about 2 mm — equals about 1 tablespoon.

What to eat peanut butter with

If you need peanut butter for nutrition but do not want to eat it plain because of the taste, texture, or boredom, pair it with something crunchy or easy to scoop.


Good options include:
  • Celery sticks
  • Carrot sticks
  • Bread
  • Saltine crackers
  • Ritz Toasted Chips
  • Wheat Thins

If cutting vegetables, taking out bread, or preparing a plate feels like too much work, use grab-and-scoop foods that come in bags or boxes, such as crackers or toasted chips.

Eat it sanitarily

To keep the peanut butter clean, avoid scooping directly from the jar with food you have already touched or bitten.

Instead, put some peanut butter into a small bowl or onto a plate, then dip or scoop from there. This keeps crumbs, saliva, and germs out of the main peanut butter jar.

Cleaning tip

You will still need to clean the spoon afterward, but it is much easier than cleaning a measuring tablespoon because a regular spoon is usually less deep and less concave. To clean it well, you may need to dip it into a cup of water with one pump of soap, then remove the spoon and scrub it off, then place back into the soapy water, and then let it sit briefly between scrub off's. After three rounds, your spoon should be pretty clean. If using your bare hands, your hands will feel oily but it's easier to remove the oil from your hands than if you were to clean the spoon directly without the dipping in soap water and waiting.

notes.label: Jamba Juice Smoothie
notes.note: You want to pair fats with your smoothie to absorb fat-soluble Vitamins A, D, E, K. For 1 cup of carrot juice, eat about 1 teaspoon of peanut butter minimum.

notes.label: Salad Kit
notes.note:
A chopped salad kit from Ralphs or Trader Joe’s usually contains several cups of salad. For one meal, about 1.75 to 2 cups is a good serving size, especially if you add bread or another side.

The dressing can be portioned out separately. After opening, you can store the small dressing packet in a snack-size Ziploc bag to prevent leaks. The smaller topping bags can usually stay inside the salad kit without issue because they are dry and less likely to spill like liquid dressing.

To keep the main salad bag closed and fresh, fold it over and secure it with a rubber band.

notes.label: Multivitamin (Adjusted)
notes.note:
These Multivitamin entries are absorption-adjusted. The numbers are not the amounts printed on the label — they estimate what your body actually absorbs from one tablet. You never absorb 100% of a pill, so water-soluble vitamins, bulky minerals (calcium, iron, magnesium, zinc), and vitamin B12 (limited by intrinsic factor) are scaled down from their label amounts.

Why these differ from the label — and why that's more accurate for you. Supplement labels follow one rule: they list how much of a nutrient is in the serving, not how much your body takes up. U.S. Supplement Facts panels are required to show the quantitative amount by weight per serving, plus a % Daily Value where one exists — and that's it. Absorption is never part of the printed number. Our adjusted values start from that label amount and then estimate what actually gets absorbed, so they line up more closely with what your body truly receives when the app totals your day.

Vitamin A is already handled — we don't double-adjust it. Vitamin A is measured in micrograms of Retinol Activity Equivalents (mcg RAE). That unit already accounts for the body absorbing and converting plant-based provitamin A (carotenoids) less efficiently than the pre-formed animal-based form (retinol). Because the FDA bakes that conversion into the Daily Value and the RAE unit, the efficiency is already reflected — so we leave it as provided rather than scaling it a second time.

Take it with a meal that has enough fat. The fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) need dietary fat to be absorbed. These values assume you ate enough fat alongside the tablet. On an empty stomach or a very low-fat meal you will absorb much less of A, D, E, and K, so the real amounts would be lower than shown.

Values are estimates of typical adult absorption and vary by person, dose, brand, and the rest of the meal. Use them as a guide, not an exact measurement.

notes.label: Parsley
notes.note: Parsley is far more than a mere garnish. This nutrient-dense herb is loaded with vitamins A, C, and K, and offers practical, targeted health benefits like supporting strong bones, flushing out excess fluids, and fighting bad breath
